_: RFC 8785 (JCS) conformance vectors - the SHARED contract.
Any implementation that produces the update-manifest signing bytes must parse
`input` with its own JSON parser, canonicalize it, and reproduce `expected`
byte for byte. Consumed by client/tests/jcs_test.gd. Issue #298, part of #69.
`refusals` is part of the contract too: these inputs must be REFUSED outright,
never serialized on a best effort.

vectors.name: rfc8785_string_escaping
vectors.why: RFC 8785 appendix string. Minimal escaping: the solidus and non-ASCII stay literal, control characters take a short form where one exists and lowercase u00xx otherwise.

vectors.name: utf16_key_order_supplementary
vectors.why: THE case that separates JCS from a code-point sort. U+10000 is the surrogate pair D800 DC00, so by UTF-16 code unit it sorts BELOW U+FFFD - the opposite of code-point order, which is exactly what Godot's JSON.stringify(sort_keys) produces.

vectors.name: key_order_basic
vectors.why: Ordering is by code unit - not case-folded, not locale-aware. The empty name sorts first, uppercase before lowercase, a prefix before its extension.

vectors.name: literals_and_empty_containers
vectors.why: Literals are bare; empty containers keep their delimiters and gain no whitespace.

vectors.name: integers
vectors.why: The manifest's number domain. Every JSON number arrives as a float in Godot, so an integral float must render with no fractional part, and 2^53 must survive exactly.

vectors.name: characters_that_stay_literal
vectors.why: JSON permits escaping these; JCS forbids it. Escaping any one of them yields valid JSON and the wrong bytes. U+007F DEL is not a C0 control and takes no escape.

vectors.name: all_short_form_escapes
vectors.why: The five control characters that have a two-character form must use it; other C0 characters must use lowercase u00xx.

vectors.name: manifest_shaped
vectors.why: A structure shaped like the real update manifest, so the vectors cover the production input and not only edge cases.

refusals.name: non_integral_number
refusals.why: RFC 8785 numbers use ECMAScript Number::toString, which this module does not implement. Guessing the bytes would sign them as if they had been verified.

refusals.name: beyond_safe_integer
refusals.why: Above 2^53 an integral float is not reliably the integer it prints as, so another implementation could not reproduce the bytes.

refusals.name: negative_beyond_safe_integer
refusals.why: The same ceiling applies on the negative side.
